ground_truth:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: Paul Robinson  DOB: 13/07/1959
MRN: 445678
Admission Date: 15/01/2024
Discharge Date: 20/01/2024
Attending: Dr. Kenneth Brown, MD, FACP

Admission Diagnosis:
Community-acquired pneumonia, right lower lobe

Discharge Diagnosis:
1. Community-acquired pneumonia, resolving
2. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
3. Essential hypertension

Hospital Course:
58-year-old male admitted with fever (102.4F), productive cough,
and right pleuritic chest pain of 3 days duration. CXR on admission
showed right lower lobe consolidation. Started on IV Ceftriaxone
and Azithromycin. Blood cultures negative. Sputum culture grew S.
pneumoniae, sensitive to Ceftriaxone. Fever resolved by day 3.
Transitioned to oral Amoxicillin-Clavulanate on day 4. Clinically
improved and discharged home.

Discharge Medications:
1. Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 875/125mg - take one tablet BID x 7 days
2. Metformin 500mg - resume one tablet twice daily
4. Lisinopril 10mg - resume once daily

Follow-up:
- Primary care in 7 days
- CXR in 6 weeks to confirm resolution

Dr. Kenneth Brown, MD, FACP
Dictated: 20/01/2024
